project.name: Big Sky Atlas
project.client_type: Fictional tourism agency (EOSYS portfolio — for sale)
project.phase_name: Execution — surgical prompts for Claude Code

objective.primary: Convert visitor into a WhatsApp or email conversation to reserve one of the hero expeditions
objective.secondary: Establish Big Sky Atlas as the most distinct premium Rockies adventure agency in the market
objective.non_goal: Ecommerce / online booking — conversion is always human-led

audience_mix.profile: Honeymoon, anniversary, milestone experience as gift
Seeking real technical challenge and credibility
Balance of comfort and adventure, multi-gen
Incentive trips, leadership offsites, team expeditions

unique_elements.desc: Type scale 160-200px on hero — unprecedented in premium travel niche
Full-bleed Mapbox GL JS v2 with real terrain, scroll-driven route reveal, POI overlays, elevation graph
Khand condensed 12px overline with expedition metadata persistent across all pages
Custom SVG cursor that rotates subtly based on scroll position and active section
1.2s parallax page transitions — images reveal as if descending a route

Daylight-dominant (70% papel). Noite-alpina reserved for 3 strategic moments: Ethos opening manifesto, Trail 3D map inside expedition template, Master map on home. Transitions between modes are 1000ms crossfades.

tech_stack.3d_terrain: Mapbox GL JS v2 (terrain source + scroll-driven camera)
tech_stack.forms: React Hook Form + Zod + Resend (email) + WhatsApp Business deeplink
tech_stack.cms: Static MDX (no CMS — boutique fixed catalog)

expeditions.name: Teton Traverse
expeditions.subtitle: Seven days across granite, three nights above timberline
expeditions.rationale: Flagship — positions the agency's technical credibility

expeditions.name: Wind River High Route
expeditions.subtitle: Nine days off the map
expeditions.rationale: Hardcore badge — for the guest who wants to earn it

expeditions.name: Beartooth Summits
expeditions.subtitle: Six days on the plateau
expeditions.rationale: Accessible challenge — most photogenic for website hero

expeditions.name: Snake River Whitewater
expeditions.subtitle: Four days of moving water
expeditions.rationale: Entry point — families, corporate incentives, groups

seo.title_tag: Big Sky Atlas — Expeditions in the Northern Rockies
seo.meta_description: A boutique agency running five technical expeditions across Montana and Wyoming. Small groups. Local guides. No shortcuts.

pages.page_entry_motion: 1200ms cinematic — hero video fades from 0 → 1 while overline and display text split-reveal in stagger

sections.objective: First impact — establish territory, tone, and scale in 3 seconds
components: Full-bleed autoplay drone video (muted, looped, poster image)
Display headline 200px — split into 2 lines, staggered reveal
Technical overline — persistent brand signature
Scroll-down cue with compass micro-rotation
Minimal nav overlaid — transparent on hero, solid on scroll
sections.differentiator: 200px display on a tourism site — first chromatic choc

sections.objective: Explain what Big Sky Atlas is in 3-4 lines without vendor-speak
components: Display 120px manifesto across 3 lines
Body supporting copy 20px
No CTA
sections.differentiator: Typography is the design — almost no visual chrome

motion.entry: ScrollTrigger reveal — each line fades as it enters viewport

sections.objective: Present 5 hero expeditions in a horizontally-scrollable editorial format
components: 5 cards in horizontal scroll (snap, drag, arrow controls)
Each card: cover image (vertical 3:4), expedition number overlay, name in Gambarino 48px, overline with duration/intensity/region, short pull quote
Progress indicator at bottom — shows '01 / 05 · 04 / 05'
sections.differentiator: Horizontal snap scroll + asymmetric cards — KOTA-inspired but editorial

motion.scroll: Horizontal pin + drag · Snap between cards · Cursor custom becomes 'drag' indicator

sections.objective: Show the territory visually — all 5 routes on Montana/Wyoming terrain
components: Mapbox GL JS v2 terrain view (zoom 7, pitch 45°)
5 route lines color-coded by expedition (musgo for contemplative, pinheiro for technical, brasa for whitewater)
Hover = route highlights + tooltip with expedition name
CTA overlay 'See all expeditions →'
sections.differentiator: This IS the killer moment — first time anyone sees the 3D map

motion.entry: Camera flies in from far away to final pitch on scroll-in · Routes draw on with staggered 400ms dashoffset animation

media.mapbox_style: custom satellite + terrain exaggeration 1.5 + atmospheric fog

sections.objective: Bridge to the Our Ethos page — one strong pull quote
components: Pull quote 56px italic Gambarino
Attribution in Khand overline
CTA 'Read our ethos' → /ethos
sections.differentiator: Contemplative pause between two energetic sections

motion.entry: Type split-char reveal on scroll-in, 40ms per char

media.background: noite-alpina with subtle Three.js noise texture

sections.objective: Build credibility with testimonials and press mentions
components: 3 testimonial cards horizontal — guest photo, quote, name/origin/expedition
Below: logo band of fictional press — 'As featured in: Condé Nast Traveler · Travel+Leisure · Outside · Nat Geo'
sections.differentiator: Cards are asymmetric — vary in height and offset

sections.objective: Convert to contact
components: Headline 'Ready to meet the mountains?' 96px
WhatsApp button (primary, brasa)
Email form inline (single field)
sections.differentiator: One of two CTAs on the page — concentrated intent

sections.objective: Legal, nav, brand signature
components: 4 columns: Expeditions · About · Contact · Follow
Address: Big Sky Atlas HQ — 214 W Main, Bozeman MT 59715 (fictional)
Certifications row: Leave No Trace · AMGA · Relais & Châteaux equivalent (fictional)
Copyright + credits

seo.meta_description: Five technical expeditions across Montana and Wyoming. From four-day river runs to nine-day traverses. Small groups, local guides.

sections.objective: Establish page + present filter system
components: H1 160px 'Expeditions'
Overline 'Five ways into the Northern Rockies'
Filter bar (see next section)
sections.differentiator: Filters are inline with the title, not hidden

sections.objective: Allow quick filtering without leaving the page
components: Intensity: 1-5 pill toggles
Duration: '3-5 days' / '6-8 days' / '9+ days' toggles
Profile: 'Couples' / 'Adventurers' / 'Families' / 'Groups' toggles
Active filter count + reset button
sections.differentiator: Filters change cursor form on hover — compass pointing at option

motion.interaction: Cards animate in/out with 400ms ease-natural, no layout shift

sections.objective: Show all 5 expeditions in editorial grid
components: Asymmetric grid (not uniform) — some cards larger, some offset
Each card: cover 4K, number, name Gambarino, overline metadata, price 'From $X', 'View expedition →' CTA
sections.differentiator: Cards on hover: image zoom 1.05x + shadow-hero applied + compass cursor highlights

motion.entry: Stagger reveal 150ms apart as user scrolls

sections.objective: Full 3D map of all 5 routes — deeper than home teaser
components: Full-height Mapbox GL JS v2 with terrain + atmosphere
All 5 routes visible with legend
Hover route = camera flies to its bounds + side panel with expedition summary + CTA
Elevation ticker shows real-time elev as cursor moves along a route
sections.differentiator: Core moment of the experiences page — prolonged exploration encouraged

sections.objective: Catch guests who don't fit the boutique catalog
components: Headline 'Not quite what you had in mind?' 72px
Body: 'Custom expeditions designed around your group'
CTA → /contact
sections.differentiator: Contrasts the boutique concept with openness

sections.objective: Cinematic first impression of THIS specific expedition
components: Full-bleed drone video of the specific route
Expedition number 'EXPEDITION 04' in Khand
Name in Gambarino 180px
Subtitle in italic 40px
Overline strip: '7 DAYS · WYOMING · INTENSITY 4/5 · FROM $11,800'
sections.differentiator: Every expedition gets the same treatment — it's the heroes, not the agency, that speak

sections.objective: THE killer moment — visitor literally walks the trail
components: Full-bleed Mapbox GL JS v2 with real terrain of the route region
Scroll-driven camera that follows the trail from start to finish
POIs appear as user scrolls — trailhead, camp sites, summit, landmarks
Each POI: small card with image + overline '3,842m · Day 4 · Camp 3'
Side panel (sticky): elevation graph in real-time + total gain/loss counter
'Explore the full map ↗' CTA opens expanded view
sections.differentiator: No other tourism site in the territory has this — primary reason for return visits

motion.scroll: Pinned section — camera flies along route at 1200ms per segment
motion.elevation_graph: Dot moves with scroll, graph shape is always visible

media.mapbox_style: custom per-expedition with specific terrain exaggeration
media.poi_images: 6-8 per expedition, generated by Nano Banana Pro
media.route_data: GeoJSON with elevation profile (fictional but plausible)

sections.objective: Editorial narrative of the itinerary, day by day
components: Vertical timeline — one row per day
Each row: 'DAY 01' overline + day title in Gambarino 48px + narrative body 560px max + thumbnail 1200px wide
Alternating alignment (odd days left, even days right) for reading rhythm
sections.differentiator: Reads like a magazine article, not an itinerary table

motion.entry: Each day reveals on scroll with 600ms ease-cinematic

media.images: 1 image per day — 7 to 9 per expedition

sections.objective: Immersive photo experience
components: Asymmetric masonry grid
12-16 photos per expedition
Click = PhotoSwipe v5 lightbox
No captions on grid — captions in lightbox
sections.differentiator: Grid is never uniform — respects image orientation, plays with scale

media.images: 12-16 per expedition, mix of landscape, portrait, detail

sections.objective: Humanize the expedition — introduce the team
components: 2-3 guide cards
Each: portrait, name, role, home mountain range, certifications, short quote
sections.differentiator: Portraits are editorial not corporate — in the field, not headshots

media.portraits: 2-3 fictional guide portraits per expedition (can reuse across)

sections.objective: Technical breakdown of what's in / what's needed
components: Two-column layout
Left: 'Included' — list with Khand overline + check icons
Right: 'What to bring' — list with Khand overline + minus icons
Bottom row: 'Provided gear' expandable — tents, packs, technical gear
sections.differentiator: Checklist aesthetic — feels like an expedition prep sheet

sections.objective: Proof from someone who did THIS specific expedition
components: Large pull quote 36px italic
Guest name, origin, date of expedition
One accompanying editorial photo
sections.differentiator: Contextual proof beats generic proof

sections.objective: Technical metadata block — price, dates, spots
components: Price: 'From $11,800 per person' in Gambarino 56px
Group: 'Maximum 6 guests' with overline
Upcoming departures: list of 3-5 dates with availability indicator (dots)
Deposit & cancellation line in body
sections.differentiator: Dates appear as instrument readings — not marketing

motion.entry: Number counter animation from 0 to price

sections.objective: Convert to WhatsApp conversation with expedition context
components: Large CTA: 'Reserve this expedition'
WhatsApp Business deeplink with pre-filled message: 'Hi Big Sky Atlas — I would like to reserve the {name} expedition ({duration_days} days, departing {next_departure}). Please advise availability.'
Email alternative below
Response time guarantee: 'Response within 4 hours · Mon-Fri'
sections.differentiator: Pre-filled WhatsApp = zero friction

seo.meta_description: Small groups, local guides, and a commitment to the mountains that have made us. Here's how we work.

sections.objective: Set the tone — magazine editorial style
components: Display 240px one-line manifesto: 'The mountains come first.'
Overline beneath
sections.differentiator: Largest typography on the site — 240px

sections.objective: Long-form origin story
components: 4-6 editorial paragraphs
Interspersed with 2 inline images (full-bleed breakouts)
Drop caps on paragraph 1
sections.differentiator: Reads like an Outside magazine feature

sections.objective: State the non-negotiables
components: 4 principles, each as a vertical card
Each: number '01-04' in Khand, principle name in Gambarino 48px, explanation in body
Example principles: 'Leave no trace', 'Small groups only', 'Local guides first', 'No shortcuts'
sections.differentiator: Principles are stated, not sold — no 'we commit to' marketing speak

sections.objective: Show the fictional team at scale
components: Grid of 8-10 team members
Each: editorial portrait, name, role, home mountain range
Hover: quote appears over portrait with blur background
sections.differentiator: Editorial portraits — shot in the field, not in a studio

sections.objective: Establish credibility via fictional badges
components: Logo row: 4-6 certification badges — Leave No Trace · AMGA · Nat Geo Unique Lodges (equivalent) · Relais & Châteaux (equivalent) · 1% for the Planet
Short explanation line beneath
sections.differentiator: Badges are monochrome — feel like patches on a jacket

sections.objective: Bridge to contact
components: Headline 'Ready to meet the mountains?' 96px
CTA → /contact

seo.meta_description: Plan your expedition. WhatsApp and email. Response within 4 hours, Monday to Friday.

sections.objective: Frame the conversation
components: H1 160px 'Let's plan your expedition'
Overline 'Response within 4 hours · Mon-Fri'

sections.objective: Primary contact path
components: Large card — full width on mobile, 2/3 on desktop
WhatsApp logo (monochrome)
Text: 'Message us on WhatsApp'
Subline: 'Tell us which expedition and when — we reply within 4 hours.'
Click = opens WhatsApp Business deeplink (number: +1 406 555 0142 fictional)
sections.differentiator: WhatsApp front and center, not buried below form

motion.hover: Card lifts with shadow-hero + brasa accent activates

sections.objective: Alternative for non-WhatsApp users
components: Form fields: Name · Email · Expedition of interest (select with 5 options + 'Custom') · Ideal dates · Group size · Message
Submit sends via Resend to hello@bigskyatlas.co (fictional)
Success state: 'Received. We'll be in touch within 4 hours.'
sections.differentiator: Form is secondary visually but functionally robust

sections.objective: Ground the agency in a real-feeling place
components: Two-column: left text, right small Mapbox map (2D this time, not 3D)
Address: 'Big Sky Atlas HQ — 214 W Main, Bozeman MT 59715'
Hours: 'Mon-Fri 08:00 - 18:00 MST · By appointment'
Note: 'Most of our guides are in the field. For urgent matters, WhatsApp is fastest.'

sections.objective: Resolve common hesitations without a form submission
components: Accordion — 7 questions
Examples: 'How fit do I need to be?' / 'What's the deposit?' / 'Cancellation policy?' / 'Solo travelers?' / 'Minimum age?' / 'Gear rental?' / 'Weather contingency?'
Each answer 2-3 lines, no marketing fluff
sections.differentiator: FAQ written like an expedition briefing, not a marketing FAQ

page_transitions.name: Trail-descent
page_transitions.description: Outgoing page shifts 20vh down with fade-out; incoming page enters from top with parallax — evokes descending a trail

cursor_custom.base: SVG compass rose 24x24px — center dot + 4 cardinal strokes
cursor_custom.hover_drag: Rose becomes horizontal arrows (for featured carousel)

scroll_triggers.primary: GSAP ScrollTrigger for pinning, timeline anchoring, camera flights
scroll_triggers.reveals: Framer Motion for element entrance (simpler, viewport-based)

modes.papel_day: Default — FAF8F4 background, carvao text, alpenglow accents
modes.noite_alpina: Reserved for Ethos opening, Trail 3D map, Master map teaser

section_alternation.text_adapts: Automatic — carvao on papel, papel on noite

motion_system_global.respects: prefers-reduced-motion — all animations become instant, 3D map becomes 2D static with POI markers, cursor reverts to default

shotlist_nano_banana_pro.style_direction: Editorial, cinematic, NATURAL DAYLIGHT with preference for golden hour morning and high noon alpine. Shot on Hasselblad X2D medium format look. Muted greens, warm granite tones, fog in valleys, alpenglow on highest peaks. Humans as scale references, not subjects. NO over-processed HDR, NO saturated greens, NO blue-sky-no-clouds, NO fantasy landscapes.

home: Hero poster: Grand Teton ridge at dawn, climber silhouette for scale, 4K 16:9
5 expedition cover images — vertical 3:4, one per expedition
3 guest portraits — editorial, in the field, varied backgrounds
Ethos teaser background — atmospheric wide shot
expedition_pages: Per expedition × 5: hero poster, 6-8 POI images, 7-9 day images, 12-16 gallery, 2-3 guide portraits, 1 testimonial photo

prompt_template_shape.format: 4K, aspect ratio {16:9 | 3:4 | 1:1}, natural light, shot on Hasselblad X2D medium format, {mood: contemplative | energetic | atmospheric}, palette {#FAF8F4 + #2A3820 + #C02B0A}, humans as scale references, no text overlay, no watermark, no HDR
prompt_template_shape.never: avoid: saturated greens, blue-sky-no-clouds, tourists with selfie sticks, neon colors, fantasy landscapes, corporate headshots

shotlist_flow_videos.style_direction: Drone-only, slow movement, FIRST LIGHT atmosphere — sun hitting highest peaks while valleys still hold fog. Slow camera reveal from valley mist up to sunlit summit. 15-20s loops max, 4K, H.265 or AV1.
list: Home hero — aerial Grand Teton ridge at first light, fog in valley, sun on summit, 20s loop
Expedition 01 Bighorn — aerial meadow dawn, slow pan, dew on grass, 15s
Expedition 02 Beartooth — aerial plateau lakes at sunrise, mirror surface, 15s
Expedition 03 Wind River — aerial backcountry ridge morning light, 20s
Expedition 04 Teton Traverse — aerial ridge descent in alpenglow, 20s
Expedition 05 Snake River — aerial whitewater morning light, mist rising off water, 15s
Transitions loop — fog rising from valley at dawn timelapse, 10s

performance_budgets.js_budget_per_page: < 300KB gzipped (excluding Mapbox which is route-specific lazy-loaded)
performance_budgets.image_format: AVIF primary, WebP fallback, jpg last resort — progressive loading with LQIP
performance_budgets.video_poster: WebP at 1920px — video itself lazy-loaded after LCP

accessibility_standards.keyboard_navigation: All interactive elements reachable via Tab
accessibility_standards.reduced_motion: Respected — 3D map becomes 2D, cursor default, no parallax
accessibility_standards.screen_reader: Semantic HTML + alt on every image + aria-labels on compass cursor context

next_phases: Phase 5 — Two visual directions (A/B) for Eduardo to choose
Phase 6 — N surgical prompts for Claude Code implementation
Side track — Image prompt generation for Nano Banana Pro
Side track — Video brief for Flow
